crime.motive: Solvaine killed Eredan to prevent him from relocating the ancestor pillars, which she believed would unmoor and destroy the spirits of every Orrish ancestor

characters.name: Eredan Volke
characters.description: The community's Bone-Speaker — its highest religious authority — a gaunt man of sixty-some years with close-cropped white hair and long ink-stained fingers. He lies at the foot of the sea-stack, face upward in the grey shale, robed in the black wool of his office. His eyes are open.

characters.name: Solvaine Drest
characters.description: Eredan Volke's apprentice and designated successor, a composed woman in her late thirties with deep-set grey eyes and ink on her right thumb. She stands near the ancestor pillars inside the hall, arms folded, watching the investigator enter with an expression of careful stillness.

characters.name: Orric Vann
characters.description: The harbour-master, a broad-shouldered man in his fifties with salt-bleached hair and a weathered face mapped with old wind-lines. He meets the investigator at the dock with his arms crossed, his expression sceptical, and does not offer his hand.

characters.name: Brynn Ossler
characters.description: The community's genealogy-keeper and elder councillor, a small, upright woman of seventy who wears her authority in the set of her jaw rather than any outward sign of office. She sits surrounded by vellum ledgers and bone-bound registers, and examines the investigator for a long moment before speaking.

characters.name: Tauvek Drest
characters.description: Solvaine Drest's younger brother, a rangy man in his mid-twenties with a fisher's calloused hands and his sister's grey eyes. He works without looking up when the investigator arrives at the fish-smoking shed, and speaks quickly, as though anxious to get words out before they can be stopped.

characters.name: Maret Colne
characters.description: A stonecutter in her late twenties who found the body at dawn and raised the alarm. She has fine stone-dust in her dark hair and her hands will not be still — she turns a small chisel over and over in her fingers as she speaks, not meeting the investigator's eyes for long.

items.name: Eredan's Satchel
items.description: A dark leather satchel, stiff with dried sea-salt, its shoulder strap frayed almost through at the buckle end, lying open on the stone platform.
items.location_discovery_text: [item:Eredan's Satchel] lies open near the rope-posts.

items.name: Vellum Manuscript
items.description: A sheaf of vellum pages bound with gut twine, covered in dense script; the margins of several pages bear notes in a second, smaller hand, written in darker ink.
items.location_discovery_text: [item:Vellum Manuscript], a sheaf of vellum pages bound with gut twine, rests inside the open satchel.

items.name: Bone-Handled Carving Knife
items.description: A short knife with a yellowed bone handle, its blade nicked along one edge; the family mark of three horizontal notches is scratched into the base of the grip.
items.location_discovery_text: [item:Bone-Handled Carving Knife] rests on the top step near the mooring post.

items.name: Pilgrim's Walking Staff
items.description: A tall staff of driftwood-grey ash, its tip wrapped in worn leather, the lower third darkened with fresh mud; a small ancestor-spiral is carved into the wood just below the grip.
items.location_discovery_text: [item:Pilgrim's Walking Staff] leans against the workshop wall, its lower third dark with mud.

locations.name: Harbour Steps
locations.flavour: Salt-rimed stone steps descend to a narrow dock where a single moored vessel knocks softly against the pilings in the swell.

locations.name: Fish-Smoking Shed
locations.flavour: The low shed reeks of cold smoke and brine; bundles of dried fish hang from the rafters like pale lanterns in the grey light.

locations.name: Ancestor Hall
locations.flavour: The hall's interior is dim, its walls lined with carved bone panels — each face worn smooth by generations of touching — and the air smells faintly of rendered fat and old wax.

locations.name: Records House
locations.flavour: Every available surface is covered in ledgers, vellum rolls, and bone-bound registers; the smell of tallow and old ink is overwhelming.

locations.name: Cliff Workshop
locations.flavour: Stone dust coats every surface; half-finished ancestor pillars stand in rows against the seaward wall, their carved faces turned toward the grey sea below.

locations.name: Base of the Spire
locations.flavour: The sea-stack rises sheer and dark from a skirt of grey shale; the smell of kelp and cold salt is sharp here, and the wind comes in gusts that lean a person sideways.

locations.name: Top of the Spire
locations.flavour: The platform is exposed on three sides to the open sea; the ancestor pillars stand in a tight circle at the centre, their carved faces scoured pale by decades of salt wind.

checkpoints.label: Where did the murder take place?
answer_options: The top of the Spire of Moored Voices

checkpoints.label: When did Eredan Volke die?
answer_options: Between first bell and second bell

checkpoints.label: Why was Eredan Volke killed?
answer_options: To prevent the relocation of the ancestor pillars
To inherit the Bone-Speaker position sooner
To silence knowledge of a harbour conspiracy

clues.text: Examining [char:Eredan Volke], the investigator finds the back of the skull shattered and both legs broken at the thigh — damage concentrated on the rear and underside of the body, with the front and face unmarked. The hands show no cuts, abrasions, or broken nails. Directly overhead rises the sheer face of [loc:spire_top].

At the outer edge of the platform, the investigator finds a splintered rope-post; caught on the raw wood is a tuft of black wool, the same weight and weave as [char:Eredan Volke]'s outer robe. The post stands directly above the patch of grey shale where the body lies far below.

[char:Orric Vann] tells the investigator that the tides were running hard the night before, and that in his thirty years on this dock he has seen a half-dozen bodies come in from the sea and wash up on the shale at the base of the Reach — always in the same spot where [char:Eredan Volke] now lies.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Orric's description of where sea-drowned bodies wash up matches the body's location, suggesting Eredan may have drowned, but the body's injuries and dry clothing are inconsistent with drowning.

clues.text: [char:Brynn Ossler] tells the investigator that [char:Eredan Volke] carried a ceremonial iron maul on every Spire observance — an instrument used to drive the base-stakes of new ancestor pillars. She says it is heavy enough to knock a man down and has been used that way in old disputes, and that it was not among his effects when the body was found.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The missing ceremonial maul implies it could have been used as a weapon, but the body's injuries are inconsistent with blunt-instrument blows and are consistent with a fall.

clues.text: [char:Maret Colne] says that when she reached [char:Eredan Volke] at dawn, his lips were slate-blue and his skin was so cold she pulled her hand back. She says she has seen fishers come in from bad crossings looking less dead than he did.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Maret's description of Eredan's blue lips and extreme cold could suggest death by exposure, but is a natural consequence of lying on frozen stone in a sea-wind for several hours after death.

clues.answer: The top of the Spire of Moored Voices
clues.text: At the seaward edge of the platform, a rope-post has been splintered recently; a torn strip of black wool is snagged on the raw wood, and the stone lip just below shows a long pale scrape, the kind left by a body dragging across gritstone. On the platform nearby, [char:Eredan Volke]'s leather satchel lies open, his bone-token resting beneath the flap.

clues.answer: The top of the Spire of Moored Voices
clues.text: Inside the satchel the investigator finds a vellum manuscript and, tucked beneath it, a small carved bone-token engraved with [char:Eredan Volke]'s personal ancestor-mark — the same token that, by Orrish custom, a Bone-Speaker carries on his person at all times and surrenders only at death. The manuscript's margins bear annotations in a second hand, smaller and darker than the body text.

[char:Tauvek Drest] tells the investigator that [time:around second bell] he was awake outside the fish-smoking shed and heard raised voices carrying from the direction of [loc:ancestor_hall] — a man's voice, he thinks, though the wind off the sea made it hard to be certain.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Tauvek's account of raised voices from the direction of the Ancestor Hall suggests a confrontation there, but he admits uncertainty and the physical evidence places the crime at the top of the Spire.

clues.text: Examining the staff at [loc:cliff_workshop], the investigator finds the lower third dark with fresh mud of the kind found on the cliff paths above the workshop, and the leather tip-wrap has a small fresh tear. The ancestor-spiral carved into the grip matches the style used exclusively by Bone-Speakers.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The presence of Eredan's distinctive staff at the cliff workshop with fresh mud on it suggests he was recently there, but the staff was left days earlier and the mud is from an unrelated errand.

clues.text: In the soft mud at the base of the harbour steps, the investigator finds a clear set of boot prints — the wide-toed, soft-soled style worn by Bone-Speakers — leading toward the water's edge, with no return prints visible in the direction of the town.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The Bone-Speaker boot prints at the harbour's edge, with no visible return prints, suggest Eredan may have been at the harbour just before his death, but the prints were made during an earlier visit and the tide obscured the return tracks.

clues.text: [char:Orric Vann] tells the investigator that [time:at second bell] he watched both [char:Eredan Volke] and [char:Solvaine Drest] cross the land bridge toward [loc:spire_top] together — Eredan carrying his leather satchel, Solvaine two steps behind him. He went inside [time:shortly after] and did not see either of them come back down before he closed the dock-house door.

[char:Maret Colne] tells the investigator that she was at [loc:cliff_workshop] from [time:first bell] onward and walked past the base of the Spire on her way there; the shale was bare and the area was empty. She did not hear any sound from the Spire until [time:at dawn], when the fog thinned and she looked out and saw [char:Eredan Volke]'s body on the shale below.

[char:Brynn Ossler] tells the investigator that she heard the alarm bell rung [time:at dawn] and that until that moment she had no reason to look toward the Spire. She says firmly that no one she knows of was watching the shale at the base through the night, and that the body might have been there for any number of hours before it was seen.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Brynn's statement that no one was watching the shale all night implies the death could have occurred after dawn and the body been there only briefly, but this is her speculation and is contradicted by Maret's testimony and the witness account of both figures ascending the Spire at second bell.

clues.text: A thin layer of frost coats [char:Eredan Volke]'s outer robe and the grey shale beneath him; the sea-fog that carries that frost has been rolling in off the water [time:since before first bell] every night this season, settling on anything left in the open.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The frost on the body and stone suggests death occurred very early — before first bell when the frost first formed — but the frost accumulated after death during the hours the body lay exposed, and does not indicate the time of death.

clues.answer: Between first bell and second bell
clues.text: [char:Tauvek Drest] tells the investigator that [time:between first bell and second bell] he heard the sound of boot-steps on the cliff path above the fish-smoking shed — measured, deliberate steps, he says, not a fisher's walk — heading in the direction of [loc:spire_base].
clues.red_herring_explanation: Tauvek's account of footsteps toward the Spire between first and second bell suggests the fatal encounter occurred in that window, but Maret confirmed the area was empty at first bell, and Orric saw both Eredan and Solvaine alive and walking together at second bell — after this window.

clues.text: When pressed further, [char:Orric Vann] says that [time:just before the alarm bell at dawn] he had come back out to check the mooring lines and saw [char:Solvaine Drest] coming down the land bridge from [loc:spire_top] alone — moving quickly, her outer robe dark at the left sleeve as though wet. He did not see [char:Eredan Volke] with her.

[char:Brynn Ossler] examines the manuscript and without hesitation points to the marginal annotations — three passages marked with small, tight script in darker ink — and states that the handwriting is [char:Solvaine Drest]'s, identical to the doctrinal submissions and record forms she has verified in her capacity as genealogy-keeper for the past twelve years.

Examining the knife at [loc:harbour_steps], the investigator finds the blade recently wiped but still showing a faint reddish residue in the nick along its edge; the three-notch family mark scratched into the bone grip is the mark of the Vann family, as noted on the harbour-master's own dock register hanging inside the dock-house door.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The Vann-marked knife with a reddish residue near the dock implies Orric had something to hide, but the knife was used for rope-splicing work and the residue is fish-blood; the cause of death is inconsistent with a knife.

clues.text: [char:Maret Colne] admits to the investigator that [char:Eredan Volke] had promised her an acolyte appointment on three separate occasions over two years and that no appointment ever came; she says she was angry enough to confront him about it. She also admits that before she ran to raise the alarm, she opened the ceremonial box at the base of the Spire and looked inside.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Maret's documented grievance against Eredan and her admission of tampering with the ceremonial box before raising the alarm create the appearance of guilt, but she was at the cliff workshop during the critical window and had no access to the Spire at the time of death.

clues.text: [char:Brynn Ossler] tells the investigator that she had argued with [char:Eredan Volke] about a doctrinal matter for more than a year, and that in their last conversation she told him she would not permit his plan to proceed while she drew breath. She pauses, then adds that she did not see him again after that conversation.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Brynn's fierce stated opposition to Eredan's plans and her cryptic final-conversation remark create strong grounds for suspicion, but she was at home during the time of death and had no access to the Spire at the relevant hour.

clues.answer: To prevent the relocation of the ancestor pillars
clues.text: The manuscript's title page reads: 'On the Necessary Relocation of the Moored Voices, being a theological argument for the removal of the ancestor pillars from [loc:spire_top] to the inland meadow, composed by Eredan Volke, Bone-Speaker of Greyspine Reach.' The body of the text argues that the encroaching sea will claim the land bridge within a generation and that the ancestors must be moved to survive.

clues.answer: To prevent the relocation of the ancestor pillars
clues.text: When the investigator asks [char:Solvaine Drest] about the relocation of the ancestor pillars, she says: 'The ancestors cannot be moved. They are not stones — they are tethered here, to this rock, to this sea. To move them is to murder them, and every living soul who carries their blood. I told him this. I told him.' Her voice does not waver and she does not look away.

clues.answer: To inherit the Bone-Speaker position sooner
clues.text: On the hall's formal notice-board, the investigator finds a sealed succession parchment bearing both [char:Eredan Volke]'s seal and [char:Solvaine Drest]'s name: a written declaration naming her as his successor to the Bone-Speaker's office, to take effect upon his retirement or death, witnessed and dated two years prior.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The succession document confirms Solvaine stood to assume the Bone-Speaker role upon Eredan's death, implying a motive of personal gain, but she had already been formally named successor and would have inherited the position naturally at his retirement.

clues.text: [char:Maret Colne] tells the investigator that last season at [loc:cliff_workshop], in her presence, [char:Eredan Volke] told [char:Solvaine Drest] that her approach to the faith was 'a mask worn over ambition' and that he had begun considering whether she was the right successor. Solvaine went pale and left the workshop without speaking.
clues.red_herring_explanation: Eredan's public suggestion that Solvaine might not be the right successor implies a personal grudge as motive, but the true motive was Solvaine's doctrinal conviction about the fate of the ancestors, not wounded pride.

clues.text: [char:Tauvek Drest] tells the investigator that for several months there had been rumours among the fishing families that his sister [char:Solvaine Drest] had broken the celibacy vow required of an apprentice Bone-Speaker, and that [char:Eredan Volke] had reportedly told a council elder he intended to review her succession on grounds of doctrinal impurity.
clues.red_herring_explanation: The rumour of a broken celibacy vow and a threatened succession review implies Solvaine killed to protect her position and conceal a personal scandal, but this is Tauvek's secondhand speculation and the true motive was doctrinal conviction about the ancestors.

leads.text: [char:Maret Colne], the stonecutter who raised the alarm, has returned to [loc:cliff_workshop] after finding [char:Eredan Volke]'s body on the shale below [loc:spire_base] — she is the only person known to have been on the clifftop at dawn.

The pre-dawn ancestor observance is performed by the Bone-Speaker and his apprentice on the platform at [loc:spire_top], above the circle of ancestor pillars — it was the last known ritual [char:Eredan Volke] was alive to conduct.

[char:Orric Vann] the harbour-master keeps irregular hours and was working on the dock before the alarm was raised; he has a direct view of the land bridge that connects the clifftop to [loc:spire_top].

opening_narrative:
Word reached the mainland in the early hours: [char:Eredan Volke], Bone-Speaker of [loc:spire_base] — the highest religious authority in the Orrish settlement of Greyspine Reach — had been found dead at the foot of the sacred sea-stack at dawn. The Elder Council, unwilling to place the matter in the hands of any local party, sent an urgent summons for an outside investigator. The message was brief and stated only that the Bone-Speaker was dead, that the manner was unclear, and that the community was afraid.

The investigator arrives [time:by mid-morning] aboard the supply vessel, stepping off the boat onto the salt-rimed harbour steps of Greyspine Reach for the first time. The settlement is a handful of stone buildings clinging to a clifftop above a grey and restless sea. The fog has not fully lifted. No one has come to meet the boat except [char:Orric Vann], who stands on the dock with his arms folded and the look of a man who has already decided the visit will come to nothing good.

The body has not been moved. The Elder Council has ordered everyone to stay in place and to speak plainly to the investigator. [char:Solvaine Drest], the dead man's apprentice, was the one who reported the death to the council [time:at dawn]; she is in the Ancestor Hall. The stonecutter [char:Maret Colne], who found the body when the morning fog lifted, has returned to the cliff workshop. The community waits.
epilogue:
[char:Eredan Volke] had watched the sea take the clifftop for forty years. As a young apprentice he had helped carry the outer pillars back from the edge after a winter storm; as a middle-aged Bone-Speaker he had ordered the land bridge reinforced twice. In his final decade he had begun corresponding with a theologian on the mainland — a man with no Orrish blood but a scholar's interest in the faith — who agreed with his assessment: within two or three generations, [loc:spire_top] would be an island, and then it would be nothing. Eredan had not been abandoning the ancestors. He had been trying to save them.

[char:Solvaine Drest] had found the manuscript draft on the lectern in the ancestor hall two months before his death. She had read it over three nights, annotating it not out of rage but out of the deep and patient effort of a person trying to find a flaw in an argument they were horrified to find convincing. She had found none. What she had found instead was a certainty she could not argue away: that the Orrish theology she had given her life to held that the ancestors were not portable — that their essence was bound to the specific stone and salt and cold, and that to move them was not relocation but extinction. She had gone to Eredan and told him this. He had listened, thanked her, and told her he intended to put the proposal to the council at the winter solstice. That conversation had been three weeks before his death.

The community did not relocate the pillars. The land bridge was reinforced a third time and then a fourth. [char:Brynn Ossler] lived long enough to see the first storm take a piece of the seaward edge of the platform. [char:Orric Vann]'s harbour ledgers, examined after the trial, showed the harbour-dues dispute had been resolved in Eredan's favour two years before his death — the animosity was old habit by then, not live grievance. [char:Maret Colne] was eventually appointed an acolyte by the council that assumed authority after the Bone-Speaker's office went unfilled, though the appointment meant little without anyone to serve. Within a generation, as Eredan had foreseen, the land bridge became impassable at high tide. The ancestors of Greyspine Reach are still there, on their platform above the cold sea, unvisited and unmoved.
